column11: definition
State where forward and reverse reactions occur at equal rates with no net change in concentrations
Principle stating that a system at equilibrium shifts to counteract any stress applied to it
Value calculated from current concentrations or pressures to predict direction of shift toward equilibrium (Q)
Equilibrium constant expressed in terms of molar concentrations for a reversible reaction
Equilibrium constant expressed in terms of partial pressures for gaseous reactions
Solubility product constant — equilibrium constant for the dissolution of a sparingly soluble ionic compound
Decrease in solubility of a salt when one of its ions is already present in solution (Le Châtelier shift)
Equilibrium where all reactants and products are in the same phase
Equilibrium where reactants and products are in different phases
Organized table used to calculate equilibrium concentrations from initial amounts and K
Substance that increases the concentration of H+ (or H3O+) ions in aqueous solution; proton donor
Substance that increases the concentration of OH- ions in aqueous solution; proton acceptor
Acid that produces H+ ions in water
Base that produces OH- ions in water
Proton (H+) donor in an acid-base reaction
Proton (H+) acceptor in an acid-base reaction
Substance that can act as either an acid or a base
Two species that differ by one H+; the acid and its corresponding base
Species formed when an acid donates a proton
Species formed when a base accepts a proton
Measure of acidity; pH = -log[H3O+] or -log[H+]
Measure of basicity; pOH = -log[OH-]
Acid that completely dissociates in water
Acid that only partially dissociates in water
Base that completely dissociates in water
Base that only partially dissociates in water
Equilibrium constant for the dissociation of a weak acid; Ka = [H3O+][A-]/[HA]
Equilibrium constant for the dissociation of a weak base; Kb = [BH+][OH-]/[B]
-log Ka; lower pKa means stronger acid
-log Kb; lower pKb means stronger base
Solution where [H3O+] = [OH-] and pH = 7 at 25°C
Solution where [H3O+] > [OH-] and pH < 7
Solution where [OH-] > [H3O+] and pH > 7
Solution that resists large changes in pH when small amounts of acid or base are added; usually contains a weak acid and its conjugate base
The actual form of the hydrogen ion in aqueous solution
Self-ionization reaction of water: 2H2O ⇌ H3O+ + OH-
Percentage of acid molecules that dissociate in solution; ( [H3O+] / initial [HA] ) × 100% for weak acids
